sections.paragraph:
νέρτερος, α, ον (cp. ἔνερθε ‘from beneath’; Trag. et al.) comp. without a positive pert. to being lower on a scale of extent, lower, nether usu. in positive mng. belonging to the lower world (τὰ) νέρτερα the underworld (Orph. Hymns 3, 10; 57, 2; 78, 5 Qu.) νερτέρων ἀνεκδιήγητα κρίματα the indescribable verdicts of the underworld 1 Cl 20:5 (on the text s. ἀνεκδιήγητος).—DELG s.v. ἔνερθε(ν).

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

comp. comp. = comparative(ly)

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

usu. usu. = usually

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

Orph. Hymns Orph. Hymns = Orphic Hymns, s. Orphica

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 669
